Name: Nile Tilapia
Primary feed source: Plant-based pellets; tolerates Spirulina, BSFL, fermented inedible biomass
Source citations: https://www.researchgate.net/figure/Nutrient-content-composition-of-nile-Tilapia-in-100-g_tbl1_362628135; Fry 2018 (Table 1); FAO 2019 (UVI, stocking densities); Pai 2024; Felix-Cuencas 2021 (flagged); USDA FDC 175169

Name: Rainbow trout
Primary feed source: High-quality formulated feed (currently fishmeal/oil; plant + insect substitution <=50% feasible)
Source citations: https://www.verywellfit.com/rainbow-trout-nutrition-facts-and-health-benefits-4801177; Torrissen 2011 (100kg feed->65kg fillet); Fry 2018 (FCR 1.2-1.5); Davidson 2016; Calabrese 2017 (75kg/m3 welfare density - flagged); USDA FDC 175167

Name: Common Carp
Primary feed source: Omnivorous; accepts formulated pellets, plant protein substitutes, kitchen waste
Source citations: https://foodstruct.com/food/carp; FAO 2019 (20-168 kg/m3 extreme robustness); USDA FDC 173687

Name: Channel catfish
Primary feed source: Carnivorous; formulated feed with fishmeal/oil; partial plant substitution OK
Source citations: https://www.nutrition-and-you.com/channel-catfish.html; Fry 2018 (FCR ~1.2-1.5); USDA FDC 173688

Name: European Seabass
Primary feed source: Estimated formulated feed / biomass-loop compatible ration
Source citations: https://www.humanitas.net/wiki/european-sea-bass/; Estimated placeholder source; user supplied candidate row

Name: Arctic Char
Primary feed source: Strictly herbivorous; eats leaves, stems, aquatic plants - primary inedible-biomass converter
Source citations: https://www.fortunefishco.net/Assets/ff%20arcticchar.pdf; Li 2022 grass carp review (only herbivorous fish that accepts whole leaf material). USDA FDC limited.

Name: Yellow Perch
Primary feed source: Estimated formulated feed / biomass-loop compatible ration
Source citations: https://www.fortunefishco.net/Assets/ff%20perch,%20yellow%20lake.pdf; Estimated placeholder source; user supplied candidate row

Name: Solein (Single-cell protein biomass)
Primary feed source: Estimated formulated feed / biomass-loop compatible ration
Source citations: https://nutriscan.app/calories-nutrition/solein-protein-powder; Estimated placeholder source; user supplied candidate row

Name: Fresh Water Prawn (Macrobrachium rosenbergii)
Primary feed source: Estimated formulated feed / biomass-loop compatible ration
Source citations: https://foods.fatsecret.com/calories-nutrition/generic/prawns?portionid=320904&portionamount=100.000; Estimated placeholder source; user supplied candidate row

Name: Fresh Water Snail
Primary feed source: Estimated formulated feed / biomass-loop compatible ration
Source citations: https://foods.fatsecret.com/calories-nutrition/usda/snails?portionid=63556&portionamount=100.000; Estimated placeholder source; user supplied candidate row

Name: Neocaridina Shrimp
Primary feed source: Omnivorous; formulated feed; can graze biofilm in biofloc systems
Source citations: https://foodstruct.com/food/shrimp-nutrition; Williamson 2019 (22% N retention); biofloc-RAS commercial standard; USDA FDC 175179

Name: Oysters Crassostrea gigas
Primary feed source: Omnivorous detritivore; scavenges; supplemental pellets
Source citations: https://www.mealstack.io/foods/mollusks-oyster-pacific-raw; FAO Cultured Aquatic Species. Limited verified nutrient data.

Name: Mussels
Primary feed source: Estimated formulated feed / biomass-loop compatible ration
Source citations: https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/food-details/174217/nutrients; Estimated placeholder source; user supplied candidate row

Name: Artemia
Primary feed source: Filter feeder: microalgae, yeast, RAS detritus
Source citations: Brown 2021 (NASA SAS palatability: remarkably similar to ground beef in texture and taste); biosecurity via salinity

Name: Yellow Meal Worms
Primary feed source: Filter feeder: algae (incl. Chlorella, Scenedesmus), bacteria from RAS
Source citations: Herawati 2018 (75% protein dry-wt); Proulx 1985 (48,000 ind/L); Gorzelnik 2023 (algae feed). CERAS heritage.

Name: House Crickets
Primary feed source: Filter feeder: microalgae (Nannochloropsis), yeast
Source citations: Yoshimatsu 2014 (160,000 ind/mL); standard hatchery first-feed for marine fish larvae

Name: Japenese Quail
Primary feed source: Filter feeder: phytoplankton
Source citations: Stottrup 2003 (cult. for marine larviculture); only needed if salmon/cod fry are reared from egg
